Weapon available to human players. Doesn't require ammo, Reverse shot and combo attacks, but it is only short ranged.

descriptions.value:
Not dead yet, but not quite alive either. 

"How did I even get here?"

Beth finally meets someone who believes in her.

"Hey man, don't mention it. That's called support."

She watched the world destroy her husband. Will her daughter meet the same fate?

"Please don't ever give up."

Is it an illness? A feeling? The weight of the world? Whatever it is, it won't stop.

"I'll beat you even if you cheat!"

A glimpse of a possible future.

"I feel so...different."
Smashing through the Terrian Military City is 188 tons of fierce metal primate!
No fly zone! This wasp is anything but hive-minded. Winged flapping fury for forcing you off the oil platform, hope you brought your  cleats or your scuba gear!
The itsy bitsy spider crawled up the water spout and dropped explosive bombs to destroy the bridge across the reservoir!
This city rampaging robo reptile rips through rebar and flattens buildings to their foundations!
Terrible tracks can't trip up this tormented train! If you're stuck on a trellis chased by this train, there won't be a Wil Wheaton in town that could save you, Vern!

descriptions.value:
Maria was once a working girl, climbing the ranks in the business with a winning combination of exquisite beauty and street smarts. Now madam of the best "den of relaxation" in the Caribbean (a term she prefers over the less classy "whorehouse"), Maria has proven herself to be a shrewd and
ruthless businesswoman. She has a warmer side, but only a few people have ever experienced it, Christopher Raven among them. Their relationship may appear to be strictly business, but there is a bond of loyalty between Maria and Christopher that goes deeper than one born of simple convenience, making people wonder if there’s anything more to their relationship behind closed doors.

Call Pete – or "Black Pete", his moniker of choice – a dirty rat, and you’re not all that far off. He is constantly sniffing out juicy rumors for his own gain. He used to be a fairly successful small-time pirate, but he got bored with the long days at sea, and had trouble submitting to the discipline of more experienced captains. Nowadays he prefers quick and easy jobs; raiding a poorly guarded ship with a motley crew of thugs, or slitting the throat of a weary merchant closing up shop for the evening.
Pete is rarely seen without his knives in his hands. The blades are the perfect tools for  Pete to carry out his own brand of violence, and he’s been heard bragging about how much he enjoys "slicing into warm flesh". Pete openly delights in taking out his frustrations on women, despite being a regular at Maria’s brothel. The girls only tolerate him because he’s a steady customer and pays generously. But even their patience is wearing thin as Pete’s urges are growing more and more sadistic - though no one truly realizes the depth of the evil that hides behind his nasty smirk.

There’s only one man Neville has ever trusted – himself. Yet the man who now goes by the name of Kensington was once as close to Neville as anyone has ever gotten. As Neville’s right-hand man, Kensington helped the Devil’s Tines prosper, keeping their coffers full of gold and their members out of jail. A brilliant strategist, it’s no surprise that when Neville’s ambition took him to new heights, Kensington rose with him.
With Neville’s influence and his own talents, he managed to build a new life for himself in the Virgin Islands, where he’s spent years amassing wealth and land while at the same time forging powerful alliances, even managing to secure himself a seat among the Patriarchs themselves. Kensington has become so influential that his voice alone is able to keep his archrival Edward Avery from joining the organization. Brilliant and ruthless, Kensington’s days may nonetheless be  numbered now that Christopher Raven is on his trail.

Once counted among the toughest pirates on Neville’s black ship, Brady has always had a volcanic temper and a shocking capacity for violence. He was once built like a young bear – and he’s still just as hard to kill – but since Neville’s crew moved on, he’s gotten a bit flabbier, and booze has dulled his mind. The best thing that can be said about "Bloodfist Brady" is that the murderous violence he employs is simply a means to an end. Whether
he’s squeezing the life out of a sailor or cutting down Christopher’s family on Neville’s orders, Brady has never reveled in violence like his comrades - he kills when it serves his needs, and simply doesn’t think twice about the lives he takes.
Like most of Neville’s men, he’s never been able to climb as high as when he sailed with the Tines. Yet, despite being on his own, he’s still managed to do well for himself as a pirate, thug for hire, and all-around professional criminal. Recently, his search for easy riches led him to an ominous-looking chain of islands – and nothing has been heard of him since.

A rich and influential man, Edward Avery is a perennial schemer who will do whatever it takes to advance his own career. He’s spent years building up his status, often clashing with Kensington to get what he wants. His greatest desire is to join the Patriarchs, a shadowy coalition of influential men who secretly control the Virgin Islands. Unfortunately for Avery, Kensington is a member of the Patriarchs, and has consistently undermined Avery’s attempts to gain entry into their ranks.
Yet when one day fate brings Avery and Christopher together, the scheming strategist – hinting that he has information not just about Kensington, but about Neville himself – feels that he can damage his foe’s status with the help of this unrefined buccaneer. They form an uneasy alliance in the hopes of destabilizing Kensington’s position, sabotaging his influence, and causing the region’s other oligarchs to lose their faith in him, eventually opening the door for Avery to join them.

A scumbag even by the standards of the Devil’s Tines, Weedy was always the first to take advantage of a helpless woman and the last to jump into a fight to help his "brothers". Debauched and gluttonous, this revolting man found himself at a loss when Neville  disappeared. Too bumbling to be a competent thief, too stupid to be a successful con-man, and too weak and inept to be hired cutthroat, Weedy ended up a pathetic has-been, hanging
around the miserable pirate hideout known as the "Brigands’ Den", where he regales all who will listen with stories of how he supposedly was once the terror of the Caribbean.
His days are filled with rum, wine, and – if he’s lucky – whiskey. His past is all that he has with him now – and his future looks to be as bleak as his surroundings, once Christopher Raven catches up with him.

Marcus is a member of the Maroons, a mysterious wandering nation made up of ex-slaves who have integrated themselves into the Caribbean world by hiring out their skills and labor, from tracking and hunting to trading and fighting. A grizzled tracker, Marcus is tougher than boiled leather and loyal to no one but fellow Maroons … until the day he finds Christopher Raven, a young boy orphaned and left for dead in a pool of his own blood.
Marcus has acted as Christopher’s surrogate father ever since, teaching him how to live honorably – as well as how to sail, how to use a sword, and how to distill rum, the beverage of choice for pirates passing the time. Now, years later, Marcus is Christopher’s friend and occasional business partner.
He’s still involved with the Maroons, but has also put down roots in St. Lucia, where he produces his potent rum.

Charlotte came to the Caribbean years ago as an innocent, wide-eyed girl. It didn’t take long for her to discover that innocence was dealt with quickly and harshly in this unforgiving world. She became a nun, seeking shelter from the cruelties of the world, choosing to serve at a remote monastery on the island of Santa Muerte. There she faithfully cares for the needy, the ill, and the downtrodden, devoting her life to a higher purpose.
Charlotte’s pious nature conceals a past fraught with pain, one that she hoped to leave behind. Little does she know that the peace she sought for so long will soon be imperiled, as Christopher Raven envelops her world like thunderclouds rolling in over calm seas.

Lancaster has a reputation throughout the Caribbean as an enigmatic figure. He’s known throughout the region as the best weapons dealer around, and his mouth is even louder than his guns. No one really knows much of anything about his past, and some suspect that "Lancaster" isn’t even his real name. He claims to be an Englishman and an aristocrat, but nobody’s ever been able to prove or disprove either of these things.
Though he’ll gladly sell weapons to anyone, he can barely conceal his hatred for Spaniards, and he’ll randomly sell faulty guns or defective powder to Spanish customers. Unprincipled as can be, but always full of cheer, Lancaster is the closest thing to a friend Christopher Raven has apart from Marcus.

Many years ago, Santorio was an up-and-coming naval officer who patrolled the Caribbean and was known for meting out swift justice to pirates. Religious and austere, he saw pirates as the living embodiment of  everything wrong with the West Indies, and everything he was trying to shield his family from. Over time, this incorruptible soldier’s hardline stance on pirates and corruption became a thorn in the side of many powerful men. The betrayal that inevitably came from his own superiors caught Santorio completely off guard, and culminated in storm of violence that left his wife and daughter dead. His faith in law and order shattered, Santorio left the navy with one goal on his mind – to kill as many pirates as he could. With a motley crew of ex-soldiers and hired mercenaries, he  became an obsessive vigilante, setting up his base of operations in an ingeniously hidden sea fortress concealed by a string of rocky formations known as "the Teeth". Hunting down pirates and paying his men with the captured spoils, Santorio is sometimes compelled to make deals with "lesser evils", but he never forgets his ultimate goal.

Neville "The Devil" Scranton is known by many names, all of them feared throughout the Caribbean. The man responsible for Christopher Raven’s rebirth as a bitter and broken man, Neville has taken so many innocent lives that he can hardly remember any one of them. They all run together, the
decades of savagery and bloodshed becoming one long and legendary spree of ruthless murder and mayhem. Neville’s dark charisma and knack for manipulation have made him a leader much better than his prowess with a blade, as well as a silent partner to the rich and powerful.
Raiding and plundering, stealing what he wants and spending his ill-gotten gains, Neville has used his brutality and his wealth to build up influence in the region so great that he could almost rule the islands himself ... an outcome that is still far from unlikely.
Christopher Raven has been called many things: a privateer, a smuggler, a pirate, a villain … but few people would ever call him a hero, and  Christopher could not care less what they think.

Christopher’s raven has been with him ever since the day his family was murdered, a constant reminder to his master of how much he’s lost. Much like his fearsome hook, the raven has become an instrument of vengeance.
Donovan is a pretty good sailor able to make any ship faster and turn a little tighter.
